subject: Sports
title: Judging Scandals
description: A short lesson to help you apply Judging Scandals.

hook.text: When humans judge sport, bias is always lurking. Some Olympic judging scandals changed entire sports.

buildup.text: At Salt Lake City 2002, a French judge admitted she was pressured to vote for the Russian pairs team.

discovery.text: The scandal led to a complete overhaul of figure skating scoring—replacing the old 6.0 system.

twist.text: Boxing has been plagued by judging controversies for decades. At Seoul 1988, a riot nearly broke out.

climax.text: Olympic sports now use technology—instant replay, sensors, and algorithms—to reduce human error.

punchline.text: Bad judging broke trust. Technology is slowly rebuilding it.

quiz.question: What did the 2002 figure skating scandal lead to?
options: A complete overhaul of the scoring system
The sport being removed from the Olympics
